sprint: Sprint 34 -- V0.27.8 Bug Fixes (deploy-fix + sync data-hygiene; all Drive-12-independent)
spec: offices/pm/issues/I-031 (US-327 backfill breaks in deploy contexts) + offices/pm/backlog/B-078 (sync_history idle churn / SyncCadenceController not idling) + offices/pm/backlog/B-079 (sync_history started_at/completed_at TZ mismatch) + offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-051 (orphan-cleanup.service IO priority -- the durable complement to V0.27.7 US-330's race-guard)
backlogItem: Bug-fix sprint #7 in the V0.27 chain. Per CIO 2026-05-12 'is there anything that can be done independent of Drive 12? if yes build that sprint' -- 4 stories that need NO IRL drive/drain to validate (synthetic tests + deployed-Pi/server queries suffice). Per chain-end-merge rule: V0.27.8 branches FROM sprint/sprint33-bugfixes-V0.27.7 (cumulative chain), NOT from main. B-077 (connection_log chatter) deferred to V0.28 per the tester's lean (it's ~1.5 rows/min post-V0.27.7, down from the 6/min reading, partially covered by V0.27.6 US-325); the one-time connection_log + sync_history truncates ride B-076's cleanup step.

testBaseline.note: Post-V0.27.7 baseline (V0.27.6's 4161 + ~43 new tests across US-326/327/328/330). Verify exact count at sprint start. PRE-EXISTING ruff drift in tests/pi/regression/test_powersource_module_identity.py (8 UP037/F401 findings, V0.24.1 file) -- ruff-version drift since baseline; not in any sprint scope; flagged repeatedly since Sprint 32.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Close 6 bugs that don't need Drive 12 or Drain 18 to validate. (1) I-031 / US-331: V0.27.7 US-327's deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 backfill mechanism fires but the backfill script itself fails in BOTH practical run-contexts -- run from the Windows dev box, Git-Bash MSYS path-mangles the Pi-side sqlite path (/home/... -> C:/Program Files/Git/home/...); run directly on chi-srv-01, loadServerCreds SSHes to 10.27.27.10 which IS chi-srv-01 -> Host key verification failed. (2) B-078 / US-332 (refined per the tester's 2026-05-12 N-5 finding): sync_history is at 20,381 rows and growing ~1.5/min even engine-off -- the Pi already has `pi_state.no_new_drives` (the flag CIO wants) but it's written-not-consumed AND reads 0 even when caught up; wire it into the sync trigger so a sweep short-circuits when nothing's new (no sync_history row, no POST) + fix the writer. (3) B-079 / US-333: every sync_history row has completed_at - started_at == exactly 18000s (5h CDT/UTC) -- one column local-time, the other UTC; make both UTC. (4) TD-051 / US-334: the V0.27.7 US-330 race-guard band-aids the journalctl-times-out-under-orphan-cleanup-IO symptom; the durable complement is IOSchedulingClass=idle + After=eclipse-obd.service on orphan-cleanup.service. (5) Spool Story E / US-335: Pi-side battery_health_log has 2 historical NULL-end rows (drain_event_id 1 + 9); a one-off idempotent script reconstructs their close-times from power_log stage_trigger rows; the sync UPDATE path then propagates to the server (a third orthogonal check of US-315's drive-side). (6) Spool Story F / US-336: US-322's orphan-cleanup left 199 leaked NULL-drive_id realtime_data rows (down from 61,293 -- 99.7% reduction; the 199 leaked); cluster + identify the dominant pattern + fix it at the source. ALL six validate via synthetic tests + queries against the already-deployed Pi/server (US-336's 'orphan count <= 50' final metric is the only Drive-12-touching bit, and it's a refinement not a hard acceptance).

EXECUTION ORDER (parallel; no inter-dependencies): TIER 1 (P2) -- US-331 (I-031 deploy-fix) + US-332 (B-078 sync-skip) + US-333 (B-079 TZ). TIER 2 (P3) -- US-334 (TD-051 orphan-cleanup IO) + US-335 (Spool E, Pi-side drain 1+9 backfill) + US-336 (Spool F, 199-orphan investigation). NOTE: US-334 and US-336 both touch orphan-cleanup -- US-334 owns deploy/orphan-cleanup.service (the unit's IO/ordering directives), US-336 owns any cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py / realtime_data-writer change; the changes compose. US-336 first-to-cut if iterations run short (P3, 'make-better', partly Drive-12-touching).

THEME: 'Make the V0.27.7 deploy machinery actually work + stop the idle write storms + close the leftover backfills.' V0.27.7 closed the Drive-11 analytics-tier gaps but left two operational residuals: the US-327 backfill that can't run in either real context (I-031/US-331), and the orphan-cleanup race US-330 only band-aided (TD-051/US-334). Plus the tester's + Spool's 2026-05-12 db-profiling surfaced: idle sync churn with a half-built fix already on the Pi (B-078/US-332 -- pi_state.no_new_drives), a sync_history TZ bug (B-079/US-333), two stranded historical drain rows (Spool E/US-335), and a 199-row leak past the working orphan-cleanup (Spool F/US-336). NOTE: Spool's 'Story G' (US-308 prior_boot_clean regression) is NOT a story here -- it's already shipped as V0.27.7 US-330; Spool's note (written before V0.27.7 closed) just adds extra evidence (both post-V0.27.6 boots NULL, incl. drain-18 post-boot) -- the IRL re-validation rides US-330's existing gate (next Pi reboot post-V0.27.8 deploy). Spool's Bug 4 (drain 18 unclear close) + the tester's 7-bug/8-smell findings file are V0.28 grooming -- not here. None of the 6 stories need a drive/drain to validate.

CONSTRAINTS (anti-blocker discipline + Sprint 28-33 lessons): (a) US-274 file-existence check ran during grooming. (b) US-282 commit-vs-claim verifier active. (c) feedback_pm_verify_diagnostic_premises.md: PM verified each premise empirically -- I-031 reproduced both failure contexts during the V0.27.7 deploy + a follow-up direct attempt; B-078 confirmed via sqlite3/mysql queries against the live Pi + server (20,381 rows, 15/10min idle); B-079 confirmed via the 18000s diff on every recent sync_history row. (d) feedback_pm_verify_table_names_against_code.md applied. (e) feedback_pm_run_pre_flight_during_grooming.md: PM ran the greps -- B-079's writer is in src/server/api/sync.py (also confirmed src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory); B-078's SyncCadenceController is in src/pi/sync/sync_cadence_controller.py, wired into core._maybeTriggerIntervalSync by Sprint 26 US-299, and the `pi_state.no_new_drives` flag exists Pi-side (one row, reads 0) per the tester's profiling -- Ralph re-confirms the exact trigger module + the pi_state writer at pre-flight (the network filesystem was slow during grooming so a full rg of src/pi/ timed out; disclosed, not skipped); I-031's root causes are in scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py + scripts/apply_server_migrations.py:loadServerCreds; US-335's sibling pattern is scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py + the Pi-side battery_health_log/power_log schemas; US-336's data is the 199 Pi-side NULL-drive_id realtime_data rows + the V0.27.6 US-322 cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py; TD-051's unit is deploy/orphan-cleanup.service. NOT a duplicate-check miss: confirmed US-332 (sync-skip) subsumes the original 'IDLE-engages' framing (the tester's doNotTouch on the B-053 intervals would have conflicted); US-335 (Pi-side drain 1+9) is distinct from US-331 (server-side rows 11-15 -- different rows, different side, different mechanism); US-336 (199-orphan leak) is distinct from US-334 (orphan-cleanup IO-throttle) and from US-322 (the 99.7%-working cleanup it follows up); Spool's Story G == V0.27.7 US-330, not re-added. (f) feedback_pm_main_merges_at_chain_end_only.md: V0.27.8 stays on the sprint branch; the V0.27 chain merges via /chain-validated once Drive 12 + Drain 18 are green. (g) feedback_pm_patch_version_bug_fix_sprint_pattern.md: V0.27.X = bug-fixes only; B-077 (connection_log chatter feature-ish 'slow it further') + B-076 (server schema epic) + B-074/B-075 (features) all stay V0.28+. (h) feedback_pm_validate_cli_in_cio_shell.md: US-331's fixes MUST be re-validated by running `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` Step 4.6 from the CIO's Windows Git-Bash shell (the failing context), not just synthetic tests.

GROUNDING SOURCES: V0.27.7 sprint-deploy 2026-05-12 (the Step-4.6 failure output captured live) + tester 2026-05-12 db-review note (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-tester-db-review-validation-bug-vs-techdebt.md: B-NEW-2 + B-NEW-3) + tester 2026-05-12 pi-state-hook + sync-skip story-proposal notes (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-tester-pi-state-hook-for-sync-chattiness.md + 2026-05-12-from-tester-story-proposal-sync-skip-when-no-new-data.md: N-5 -- pi_state.no_new_drives exists, written-not-consumed; the concrete sync-skip story shape) + Spool 2026-05-12 V0.27.7-addendum note (offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-spool-v0277-addendum-drive12-independent-work.md: Story E Pi-side drain 1+9 backfill; Story F 199-orphan leak; Story G = already V0.27.7 US-330 with extra evidence) + PM 2026-05-12 live re-query (sync_history 20,381 rows / 15 in 10 min idle; every row diff_s=18000; connection_log ~35k Pi-side) + V0.27.7 US-330 close note + TD-051 (the orphan-cleanup IO-priority filing) + the tester's full findings file offices/tester/findings/2026-05-12-obd2db-data-profile-additional-findings.md (7 more bugs + 8 design smells -- NOT pulled into this sprint; V0.28 grooming).

POST-SPRINT-34 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO no-human-tasks rule): (1) CIO re-runs `bash deploy/deploy-server.sh` from Windows Git-Bash post-V0.27.8 -> Step 4.6 backfill completes; server battery_health_log rows 11-15 populated; rerun no-op (US-331 / I-031 real-world gate). (2) CIO runs `scripts/backfill_pi_battery_health_log_historical_drains.py --execute` once post-V0.27.8 (US-335 / Spool E -- one-off; the script is idempotent so a repeat is a no-op). (3) CIO does a deliberate bench-drain on V0.27.8 to disambiguate Spool's Bug 4 (drain 18 had end_timestamp=NULL Pi-side -- could be a legitimate AC-restoration-before-TRIGGER, a Pi clock jump, or a close-event regression; not enough signal to file a story yet; a clean V0.27.8 drain close on both sides resolves it). (4) The V0.27 chain validation gate is unchanged: Drive 12 (full server pipeline -- drive_summary analytics + Approach-1 drive_statistics rows) + Drain 18 (clean startup_log prior_boot_clean=1). When BOTH green AND V0.27.8 is in the chain -> /chain-validated merges + cuts V0.28.0. (5) V0.28+ queue: B-074 (MAP PID) + B-075 (drive_statistics Approach 2) + B-076 (server schema normalization epic -- owns the deferred US-329 fix + DROP TABLE drive_counter + the one-time connection_log/sync_history truncates) + B-077 (connection_log chatter -- now low-urgency) + B-080 (Pi clock drift ~23h post-reboot -- Spool's Bug 5, just recurred at the drain-18 boot) + the tester's 7-bug/8-smell findings file. (6) regression_manifest updates Spool flagged (US-320/US-321/US-322 IRL-confirmed 2026-05-12) are recorded in the archived Spool inbox note; the manifest bumps formally at /chain-validated time per the chain-end-merge workflow -- not now.

stories.title: US-327 backfill works in both deploy run-contexts -- I-031
stories.intent: I-031: V0.27.7 US-327 wired deploy-server.sh Step 4.6 (idempotent backfill of stranded server-side battery_health_log rows) + a --count-stranded pre-check. The plumbing fires (it found 5 stranded rows + triggered the backfill), but the backfill script itself fails in BOTH practical run-contexts: (Context 1, run from the Windows dev box via Git Bash) the Pi-side sqlite path `$HOME/Projects/Eclipse-01/data/obd.db` gets MSYS2 path-mangled to `C:/Program Files/Git/home/mcornelison/...` when interpolated into the remote `ssh ... sqlite3 -readonly <path>` command -> 'unable to open database file'. (Context 2, run directly on chi-srv-01) `loadServerCreds` (imported from apply_server_migrations.py) SSHes to mcornelison@10.27.27.10 to read DATABASE_URL -- but chi-srv-01 IS 10.27.27.10, so this ssh-to-self fails 'Host key verification failed'. The Step-4.6 wiring is correct; the host/path resolution under real conditions is the gap. Fix BOTH: (1) the remote-sqlite3 path is no longer MSYS-mangled from Git Bash (MSYS_NO_PATHCONV / single-quote so only the remote shell expands it / build the command so the leading `/` isn't treated as a Windows drive path); (2) when the server address resolves to localhost, loadServerCreds reads DATABASE_URL from the local .env instead of ssh-to-self (or accepts the self-loop host key gracefully).

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (UPDATE -- the remote Pi-side sqlite3 invocation no longer MSYS-path-mangled from Git Bash; e.g. wrap with MSYS_NO_PATHCONV=1 / single-quote the remote command so only the Pi's shell expands `$HOME/.../obd.db` / pass the path so the leading `/` isn't a Windows-drive trigger; mod-history US-331)

scripts/apply_server_migrations.py (UPDATE -- loadServerCreds: when the server address is localhost / resolves to this host, read DATABASE_URL from the local .env directly instead of `ssh mcornelison@<self>`; or fall back to `-o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new` for the self-loop; same fix benefits Step 4.5 migrations run on chi-srv-01)

tests/scripts/test_backfill_deploy_contexts.py (NEW -- regression test: (a) the remote-sqlite3 command string is not path-mangled when MSYS_NO_PATHCONV-style guarding is in effect / the path is single-quoted; (b) loadServerCreds with a localhost-ish server address reads .env locally and does NOT shell out to ssh; both would FAIL pre-fix)
filesToRead: offices/pm/issues/I-031-us327-backfill-breaks-in-deploy-contexts.md (both failure contexts + fix candidates)

stories.title: sync-skip-when-no-new-data via pi_state.no_new_drives -- B-078
stories.intent: B-078 (refined by the tester's 2026-05-12 N-5 finding + concrete story proposal): at idle (engine off, parked on home WiFi, all local data already synced) the Pi runs a sync sweep ~every 26-40s -- 3,290 sync_history rows on 2026-05-11 alone, each carrying 1-3 rows of engine-off polling noise; sync_history is now ~20,381 rows (15 in a 10-min idle sample). The Pi ALREADY HAS the gate state for this: `pi_state.no_new_drives` (one row, currently reads 0). It's written but NOT consumed by the sync trigger, AND it currently reads 0 even though Drive 11's data is fully synced (so the writer isn't setting it true when caught up). Fix: (1) wire it into the interval-sync trigger (where Sprint 26 US-298/299's SyncCadenceController is also wired -- core._maybeTriggerIntervalSync or equivalent): before a sweep, if `pi_state.no_new_drives` is true AND every synced table's `sync_log.last_synced_id == current MAX(id)` (the cheap 'nothing new' check, confirmed by the tester's 2026-05-12 profiling pass), skip the sweep entirely -- write NO sync_history row, send NO outbound POST; (2) fix the `pi_state.no_new_drives` writer so it's set true after a successful sync that found nothing new / after the post-drive sync completes, and set false when new local data appears (drive starts, new connection_log rows). This is an orthogonal short-circuit ON TOP of the SyncCadenceController -- do NOT touch the B-053 IDLE/ACTIVE intervals themselves. The one-time sync_history prune CIO wants (~20k rows, ~8.5k of which are old failed rows -- failures stopped 2026-05-08, root causes fixed by migration 0006 + a resolved realtime_data optimistic-lock race) rides B-076's cleanup step (NOT this story).

filesToTouch: src/pi/obdii/orchestrator/core.py (UPDATE -- `_maybeTriggerIntervalSync` (confirmed here; Sprint 26 US-299 wired SyncCadenceController in here): before a sync sweep, if pi_state.no_new_drives is true AND every synced table's sync_log.last_synced_id == current max id -> skip the sweep entirely (no sync_history row, no outbound POST). ALSO the pi_state.no_new_drives writer if it lives here -- set true after a successful sync that found nothing new / after the post-drive sync completes, set false when new local data appears (drive_start, new connection_log rows). If pre-flight (`rg no_new_drives src/pi/`) finds the flag writer is in a DIFFERENT module, add that file here per the scope-fence amendment process)

tests/pi/sync/test_sync_skip_no_new_data.py (NEW -- seed a DB with all-synced state + pi_state.no_new_drives=true -> run the trigger -> assert NO sync_history row + NO outbound POST; then add a new realtime_data row -> assert the next tick syncs + writes exactly one sync_history row. Would FAIL pre-fix (the flag is unconsumed -> the sweep always runs))

ACTIVE-5s behaviour during an actual drive (no regression on drive-time sync latency)

Tester 2026-05-12 profiling pass (N-5): Pi obd.db has a `pi_state` table -- one row, `no_new_drives = 0` -- that IS the flag CIO wants for 'if sync successful and no new drive, no need to sync', but it's written and not consumed by the sync trigger; the sync still runs ~every 26s at idle (3,290 sync_history rows on 2026-05-11 alone). The flag reads 0 right now even though Drive 11's data is fully synced -- the 'set true when caught up' writer path is the missing piece.

After Drive 12 completes + syncs -> pi_state.no_new_drives flips true; subsequent idle ticks don't sync. New local data (drive start, new connection_log rows) -> flips false; the next tick syncs + writes exactly one sync_history row.

stories.title: sync_history started_at + completed_at both written in UTC -- B-079
stories.intent: B-079: every recent sync_history row has `completed_at - started_at == exactly 18000s` (5h00m00s = America/Chicago CDT offset) -- one of the two columns is written with a local-time clock, the other with UTC, within the same row. The sync-duration metric (`completed_at - started_at`) is therefore garbage (always ~5h). TD-027-class bug: a naive-timestamp writer that escaped the Sprint 14 sweep. The writer lives in src/server/api/sync.py (confirmed via grep; src/server/db/models.py:SyncHistory is the model). Fix: make both columns use UTC per the TD-027 / specs/standards.md convention (datetime.now(timezone.utc) on the Python side / canonical ISO-8601 UTC) -- whichever of started_at/completed_at is currently wrong gets flipped to match the correct one.

filesToTouch: src/server/api/sync.py (UPDATE -- the sync_history row writer: both `started_at` and `completed_at` written with the same UTC-aware clock; mod-history US-333 + TD-027 cross-ref)

stories.title: orphan-cleanup.service I/O throttle + ordering -- TD-051
stories.intent: TD-051: V0.27.7 US-330 fixed the I-030 startup_log regression with a race-guard (detectBootReason retries `journalctl --list-boots` 3x when it times out under boot-time I/O contention). The root *cause* of that contention is the V0.27.6 US-322 orphan-cleanup.service: its timer has Persistent=true (so it fires at boot to catch up the missed nightly 03:00 run) and the service runs `cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py --execute` -- a DELETE against the Pi 5 SD-card SQLite DB -- concurrently with the launching eclipse-obd orchestrator. The race-guard absorbs the symptom; the durable complement is to (a) throttle orphan-cleanup.service's I/O via `IOSchedulingClass=idle` (best-effort -> only runs when nothing else needs the disk) and (b) order it `After=eclipse-obd.service` so the boot-time catch-up DELETE doesn't starve the orchestrator's early `journalctl` calls. Low urgency (the race-guard is sufficient); this is hardening + a cleaner boot.

filesToTouch: deploy/orphan-cleanup.service (UPDATE -- add `IOSchedulingClass=idle` (and consider `Nice=19` / `IOSchedulingPriority=7`) to [Service]; add `After=eclipse-obd.service` to [Unit]; mod-history US-334. deploy-pi.sh's step_install_orphan_cleanup_unit already syncs this file if-changed -- no deploy-pi.sh change needed unless pre-flight finds the unit content is also templated there)

tests/deploy/test_orphan_cleanup_install.py (NEW -- assert the installed/synced orphan-cleanup.service contains `IOSchedulingClass=idle` + `After=eclipse-obd.service`; mirror the pattern in tests/deploy/test_drain_forensics_install.py / test_journald_persistent_install.py)
filesToRead: offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-051-orphan-cleanup-io-priority.md (the filing)

V0.27.7 US-330 close notes (the race-guard this complements)
deploy/eclipse-obd.service + deploy/drain-forensics.service (sibling units -- match their [Unit]/[Service] style; check what IOSchedulingClass/Nice settings they already use, if any)
doNotTouch: src/pi/diagnostics/boot_reason.py (the US-330 race-guard stays -- this story is the *complement*, not a replacement; belt-and-suspenders)
cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py business logic (only the systemd unit's resource/ordering directives change, not what the cleanup does)
orphan-cleanup.timer schedule (the nightly 03:00 OnCalendar + Persistent=true stay -- we're throttling the run, not changing when it's scheduled)

groundingRefs.value: V0.27.7 US-330 close note: the I-030 both-NULL startup_log row traces to `journalctl --list-boots` returning None (TimeoutExpired -- the 10s JOURNALCTL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS cap blown under boot-time SD-card I/O contention); the V0.27.5->V0.27.6 delta that fits the regression cliff is US-322's orphan-cleanup.timer (Persistent=true -> fires at boot, runs a DELETE on data/obd.db concurrently with the launching orchestrator). Filed as TD-051; the race-guard (3x list-boots retry) absorbs the symptom.

Pi deploy 2026-05-12 output confirms the orphan-cleanup unit + timer are installed + enabled: 'Installing orphan-cleanup systemd unit (US-322 / B-072, sync-if-changed) -- orphan-cleanup.service already up-to-date. orphan-cleanup.timer enabled + active.'

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.8 deploy + a Pi reboot (e.g. after Drain 18) -> the orphan-cleanup boot-time catch-up run does not delay the orchestrator's startup_log write (prior_boot_clean populated -- same as US-330's gate, just with the contention removed at the source rather than retried around); `systemctl show orphan-cleanup.service -p IOSchedulingClass -p After` reflects the new settings

acceptance: Pre-flight: locate the orphan-cleanup.service unit definition (deploy/orphan-cleanup.service or the heredoc in deploy-pi.sh); check sibling units (eclipse-obd.service, drain-forensics.service) for the existing IOSchedulingClass/Nice/ordering style to match

stories.title: Pi-side drain backfill -- drain_event_id 1 + 9 -- Spool Story E
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-12 Story E: Pi-side `battery_health_log` has 2 historical rows with end_timestamp=NULL -- drain_event_id=1 (start 2026-05-04T13:21:08Z) and drain_event_id=9 (start 2026-05-09T01:47:10Z). Per MEMORY.md: drain 9 specifically -- the Pi died mid-drain and the close-event didn't write before `systemctl poweroff` (pre-V0.27.2 root cause; V0.27.2 prevents recurrence but doesn't backfill these two). Both close-times are reconstructable from contemporaneous `power_log` `stage_trigger` rows (the timing-truth source) -- e.g. drain 1's close ~2026-05-04T13:34:09Z per the Drain Test 10 record. Fix: a one-off idempotent script under `scripts/` that populates end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds for drain_event_id IN (1,9) on the Pi from the matching power_log stage_trigger rows. The (now-fixed by US-326 + US-331) sync UPDATE path then propagates these to the server-side rows for source_id IN (1,9) -- which is also a third orthogonal validation of US-315's drive-side. Drive-12-independent (pure SQL/script + bench).

filesToTouch: scripts/backfill_pi_battery_health_log_historical_drains.py (NEW -- reads Pi-side power_log for the stage_trigger row matching each NULL-end drain_event_id (default {1, 9}), derives end_timestamp + end_soc + runtime_seconds, UPDATEs battery_health_log; idempotent (`AND end_timestamp IS NULL` guard on every UPDATE; --dry-run default + --execute; --drain-event-ids override); modeled on the structure of scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py; mod-history US-335)

tests/scripts/test_backfill_pi_historical_drains.py (NEW -- synthetic Pi DB with 2 NULL-end drain rows + matching power_log stage_trigger rows; run --execute -> both rows populated with values derived from the stage_trigger timestamps; re-run -> no-op (idempotent); a drain row that's already closed is left untouched)

scripts/backfill_server_battery_health_log_stranded.py (V0.27.7 US-327 + V0.27.8 US-331 -- the sibling backfill script's structure; mirror its idempotency + dry-run/execute + arg pattern)

src/pi/obdii/database_schema.py battery_health_log + power_log schemas (the columns: battery_health_log.{end_timestamp, end_soc, runtime_seconds}; power_log.{timestamp, stage, vcell/soc} for the stage_trigger row)

doNotTouch: Pi-side battery_health_log rows OTHER than drain_event_id IN (1,9) (and any that already have end_timestamp populated)

The server-side battery_health_log rows directly (those update via the existing US-315 sync UPDATE path after the Pi-side rows are fixed; don't write the server directly)

Drain 1 close reconstructable from the power_log stage_trigger row at 2026-05-04T13:34:09Z (Drain Test 10 record).

Real-world validation gate: post-V0.27.8, run the script with --execute against the Pi -> Pi-side rows for drain_event_id IN (1,9) have populated end_timestamp/end_soc/runtime_seconds; the (US-326+US-331-fixed) sync UPDATE path then propagates to server-side source_id IN (1,9) -- a third orthogonal confirmation of the drive-side sync UPDATE. Re-running the script is a no-op.

stories.title: Investigate the 199 leaked realtime_data orphans -- Spool Story F
stories.intent: Spool 2026-05-12 Story F: after V0.27.6 US-322's orphan-cleanup landed, Pi-side `realtime_data` NULL-drive_id orphan count is 199 -- down from 61,293 pre-cleanup (99.7% reduction; US-322 working). The 199 leaked through. Hypotheses: (a) writes that arrived during the cleanup-timer's transaction window; (b) reconnect-noise polls between the timer's firings; (c) a guard the timer doesn't cover (e.g. PIDs that always poll before DriveDetector engages). This is 'make a working solution even better,' not 'fix broken' -- Spool's analytics already filter on drive_id IS NOT NULL, so the 199 rows are inert noise, not data poison. Fix: cluster the 199 by timestamp + PID, identify the dominant pattern, and either tighten the timer / add a writer-side guard (if it's between-timer noise) OR fix the cleanup's transactional isolation / add a final sweep (if it's a transaction race). The investigation + the writer-guard/cleanup fix are Drive-12-independent; the final 'post-Drive-12 orphan count stays <= 50' check is a validation refinement that needs Drive 12.

filesToTouch: scripts/cleanup_orphan_realtime_data.py (UPDATE -- Ralph pins the fix at pre-flight after clustering the 199: if 'transaction race during cleanup' -> tighten this script's transactional isolation or add a final cleanup sweep; if 'between-timer noise' -> a tighter cleanup cadence here (coordinate with US-334 which owns the orphan-cleanup.service unit file). If the pattern instead points at the realtime_data WRITE path -- engine-off polls landing before DriveDetector engages -- add a writer-side guard in src/pi/obdii/database.py (or wherever realtime_data INSERTs happen) per the scope-fence amendment process)

tests/scripts/test_cleanup_orphan_leak.py (NEW -- regression test for whichever pattern is found: e.g. a write arriving 'between cleanup firings' is swept by a final pass / dropped at write time; would FAIL pre-fix (the row leaks past the cleanup))
filesToRead: offices/pm/inbox/2026-05-12-from-spool-v0277-addendum-drive12-independent-work.md (Story F -- the 3 hypotheses + the clustering approach)

this sprint's US-334 (also touching orphan-cleanup.service -- coordinate so the two stories don't conflict on the unit file)
doNotTouch: Spool's analytics filtering (drive_id IS NOT NULL) -- that's the correct downstream behaviour; this story reduces the noise at the source, not the filter
The 99.7%-working US-322 cleanup's core logic (only tighten/guard the leak path -- don't re-architect the cleanup)
DriveDetector's engage logic (don't change WHEN it engages; the fix is about rows written BEFORE it engages, not changing that timing)

groundingRefs.value: Spool 2026-05-12 (Story F): post-US-322, Pi-side realtime_data NULL-drive_id orphan count = 199 (down from 61,293 pre-cleanup -- 99.7% reduction; US-322 working). The 199 leaked through. 3 hypotheses: (a) writes during the cleanup-timer's transaction window, (b) reconnect-noise polls between timer firings, (c) a guard the timer doesn't cover (PIDs polling before DriveDetector engages).

Real-world validation gate: cluster the 199 by timestamp + PID -> identify the dominant pattern; after the fix + Drive 12, Pi-side NULL-drive_id orphan count stays <= 50 (a further ~75% reduction from the current 199). The clustering + the fix are Drive-12-independent; the <=50-post-Drive-12 metric is the validation refinement.

The 199 leaked realtime_data orphans are clustered + the dominant pattern identified + fixed at the source; US-322's 99.7% reduction preserved; post-Drive-12 the orphan count stays <= 50 (US-336 / Spool Story F)
